chief_complaint: Pleuritic chest pain in young woman on oral contraceptive
clinical_reasoning: 28-year-old female presenting with pleuritic chest pain for 2 days. Pain reproducible on palpation, onset after physical exertion. However, OCP use (drospirenone/ethinylestradiol) is noted in medication history — this raises the possibility of VTE-related chest pain. SpO2 95% is marginally low for a young healthy patient and tachycardia (HR 102) is noted. While musculoskeletal strain remains the most likely diagnosis, the combination of pleuritic pain + OCP use + borderline SpO2 warrants PE exclusion. Recommend D-dimer as initial screening if clinical suspicion persists. YELLOW — urgent but stable.
vital_sign_concerns: SpO2 95% — marginally low for age, warrants monitoring
HR 102 — mildly elevated, could reflect pain or underlying process
risk_factors: Prior ED visit for calf pain
